edges.description: Transformed thymic epithelium accumulates recurrent tumor suppressor alterations.
edges.target: CDKN2A and TP53 tumor suppressor loss

edges.description: Transformed cells engage RTK signaling, including KIT.
Loss of G1/S checkpoint control drives uncontrolled proliferation.
edges.source: CDKN2A and TP53 tumor suppressor loss

edges.description: RTK signaling drives proliferative output in thymic carcinoma cells.
Uncontrolled proliferation precedes aggressive invasion and spread.

edges.target: CDKN2A and TP53 tumor suppressor loss

nodes.description: Convergent loss of checkpoint control and gain of RTK signaling drives sustained, dysregulated proliferation of the malignant thymic epithelium.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic insights into thymic carcinomas and thymic neuroendocrine neoplasms denote prognosis signatures and pathways.
evidence.snippet: the prognosis-related genes were focused on signal pathways of cell cycle control, chromatin remodeling/DNA methylation

nodes.description: Avapritinib, a type 1 KIT/PDGFRA inhibitor active against KIT exon 17 mutants, has produced responses in thymic carcinoma harboring KIT exon 17 mutations, illustrating a precision-oncology strategy for the KIT-mutant subset.

evidence.reference_title: Differential activity of avapritinib in patients with metastases from mucosal melanoma and thymic carcinoma harbouring KIT exon 17 mutations: Initial experience from a Compassionate Use Program in Italy.
evidence.snippet: We describe a differential activity of avapritinib (3/4 patients responded, 1/4 did not respond)

nodes.description: The most frequent recurrent genomic alterations in thymic carcinoma involve the tumor suppressors CDKN2A (p16) and TP53, deregulating the G1/S cell-cycle checkpoint and p53-dependent damage responses and giving thymic carcinoma a higher mutational burden than thymoma.
nodes.id: CDKN2A and TP53 tumor suppressor loss

biological_processes: regulation of G1/S transition of mitotic cell cycle

evidence.reference_title: Genetic insights into thymic carcinomas and thymic neuroendocrine neoplasms denote prognosis signatures and pathways.
evidence.snippet: The genetic alterations that ranked highest in TCs were in CDKN2A, TP53, ASXL1, CDKN2B, PIK3C2G, PTCH1, and ROS1

evidence.reference_title: Insights into molecular aspects and targeted therapy of thymic carcinoma: a narrative review.
evidence.snippet: it suggests that the frequently involved genes or pathways associated with thymic carcinoma are tumor suppressor genes, including TP53 and CDKN2A/B, and the receptor tyrosine kinase pathway.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic characterization of thymic epithelial tumors in a real-world dataset.
evidence.snippet: In the FMI data, CDKN2A (39.9%), TP53 (30.2%) and CDKN2B (24.6%) were frequently altered in thymic carcinoma, versus TP53 (7.8%), DNMT3A (6.8%), and CDKN2A (5.8%) in thymoma.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic insights into thymic carcinomas and thymic neuroendocrine neoplasms denote prognosis signatures and pathways.
evidence.snippet: The genetic alterations that ranked highest in TCs were in CDKN2A, TP53, ASXL1, CDKN2B, PIK3C2G, PTCH1, and ROS1

evidence.reference_title: Genomic characterization of thymic epithelial tumors in a real-world dataset.
evidence.snippet: In the FMI data, CDKN2A (39.9%), TP53 (30.2%) and CDKN2B (24.6%) were frequently altered in thymic carcinoma, versus TP53 (7.8%), DNMT3A (6.8%), and CDKN2A (5.8%) in thymoma.

evidence.reference_title: Differential activity of avapritinib in patients with metastases from mucosal melanoma and thymic carcinoma harbouring KIT exon 17 mutations: Initial experience from a Compassionate Use Program in Italy.
evidence.snippet: Recently, KIT exon 17 mutants turned out to be a potential molecular target for the type 1 kinase inhibitor avapritinib (BLU-285).

nodes.description: Thymic carcinoma is typically invasive at diagnosis, infiltrating mediastinal structures and frequently spreading to regional lymph nodes and distant sites, underlying its aggressive clinical course and poor prognosis relative to thymoma.

evidence.reference_title: Thymic Carcinomas-A Concise Multidisciplinary Update on Recent Developments From the Thymic Carcinoma Working Group of the International Thymic Malignancy Interest Group.
evidence.snippet: These tumors are usually invasive, often present at advanced stages, and typically behave aggressively.

nodes.description: Thymic carcinoma arises from malignant transformation of thymic epithelial cells, producing overtly atypical, invasive epithelium with marked architectural and cytologic atypia, in contrast to the bland epithelium of thymoma.

evidence.reference_title: Insights into molecular aspects and targeted therapy of thymic carcinoma: a narrative review.
evidence.snippet: Thymic carcinomas are rare tumors derived from thymic epithelial cells.

evidence.reference_title: Thymic Carcinomas-A Concise Multidisciplinary Update on Recent Developments From the Thymic Carcinoma Working Group of the International Thymic Malignancy Interest Group.
evidence.snippet: Thymic carcinomas are rare malignancies that in general arise in the prevascular

nodes.description: Receptor tyrosine kinase signaling, including the KIT (CD117) receptor, is recurrently activated in thymic carcinoma. A subset of squamous cell thymic carcinomas harbors activating KIT mutations (including exon 17 mutations), providing oncogenic proliferative and survival signals and a targetable vulnerability.

biological_processes: cell surface receptor protein tyrosine kinase signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: Preferentially expressed antigen in melanoma as a novel diagnostic marker differentiating thymic squamous cell carcinoma from thymoma.
evidence.snippet: established markers for TSQCC, including KIT and CD5, which are expressed in ~ 80% of TSQCCs and ~ 3% of thymomas.

evidence.reference_title: Differential activity of avapritinib in patients with metastases from mucosal melanoma and thymic carcinoma harbouring KIT exon 17 mutations: Initial experience from a Compassionate Use Program in Italy.
evidence.snippet: Recently, KIT exon 17 mutants turned out to be a potential molecular target for the type 1 kinase inhibitor avapritinib (BLU-285).
